data.name: FirstRun_Prompt
data.value: Choose your language. You can change this later from the gear button in the top-right.

data.name: LauncherSettings_Tooltip
data.value: Change the launcher's language and other launcher preferences.

data.name: Announcements_Intro
data.value: Shown to players before a restart. Put {minutes} where the countdown should go (it becomes the minutes remaining). Remove it for a fixed message with no time.

data.name: Announcements_EmptyMessage
data.value: An announcement can't be empty (the server rejects a blank message):

data.name: Announcements_NoTokenMessage
Main_PrimaryTip
data.value: Install the server, or start (▶) / stop (■) it once installed. Start checks for an update first (when Update on start is on), then launches.

data.name: Main_RestartTip
data.value: Restart now: saves and restarts the server immediately, with no player countdown. Scheduled and update restarts warn players first.

data.name: Main_ForceShutdownTip
data.value: Immediately kill the server process, with no save. Appears when a start, stop, or restart has been stuck for a while. Use it only when the server has stopped responding.

data.name: Main_CheckForUpdateTip
data.value: Check SteamCMD for a newer build (read-only; works while running)

data.name: Main_ValidateFilesTip
data.value: Full SteamCMD integrity check of the installed files

data.name: Main_ServerSettingsTip
data.value: Edit the server in one tabbed editor: Game Settings (the PalWorldSettings.ini gameplay, balance, and admin keys as sub-tabs), Launch Arguments (the command line: listen port, player cap, performance threads, community listing), and CPU Affinity/Priority (process priority and CPU pinning).

data.name: Main_ModsTip
data.value: Manage Steam Workshop server mods: add Workshop ids for the launcher to download, or scan for mods you dropped into the mods folder yourself.

data.name: Main_PublicIpTip
data.value: Your public IP and game port. Reveal it with the eye, then highlight to copy, or use the copy button.

data.name: Main_RevealIpTip
data.value: Show or hide your public IP

data.name: Main_CopyIpTip
data.value: Copy your IP:port to the clipboard

data.name: Main_PortCheckTip
data.value: Check whether your ports are reachable from the internet. The server must be stopped so the launcher can test the ports.

data.name: Main_CollapseTip
data.value: Show or hide the settings sections (Restarts, Backups, Misc)

data.name: Main_ScheduledRestartTip
data.value: Automatically restart the server at the times listed. A restart is skipped if the server hasn't been up for the minimum uptime below.

data.name: Main_AnnounceRestartsTip
data.value: Warn players in-game before a scheduled or update restart, at the minute marks on the right. Skipped when nobody's online.

data.name: Main_AnnounceLeadTip
data.value: Minutes before restart to announce (blank = off)

data.name: Main_EditAnnouncementsTip
data.value: Edit the in-game messages players see before a restart.

data.name: Main_ValidateOnRestartTip
data.value: Add a full integrity pass to the update on Start/restart (slower)

data.name: Main_BackupStartupTip
data.value: Back up when the server starts, before it checks for updates (SteamCMD can overwrite config).

data.name: Main_BackupShutdownTip
data.value: Back up when the server stops or restarts (saves the world first).

data.name: Main_BackupScheduledTip
data.value: Automatically back up at the times listed on the right, while the server is running.

data.name: Main_BackupRetentionTip
data.value: How long to keep automatic backups (startup / shutdown / scheduled). Manual backups and any file you add to the backups folder yourself are never deleted. 0 = keep everything.

data.name: Main_BackupNowTip
data.value: Take a manual backup immediately. Saves the world first if the server is running with REST on.

data.name: Main_AutoUpdateTip
data.value: Automatically restart the server when a new update is found. The Announce restarts setting adds a delay before restarting if anyone is on the server.

data.name: Main_UpdateOnStartTip
data.value: Run a SteamCMD update check each time the server starts or restarts. Uncheck to never auto-download on start. You can still update explicitly via Check for Update.

data.name: Main_CheckUpdateEveryTip
data.value: How often to check SteamCMD for a new build while the server is running. Applies on next start.

data.name: Main_HideSteamCmdTip
data.value: Run SteamCMD in the background instead of opening its own console window. Live progress still appears in the SteamCMD tab.

data.name: Main_LogServerStatusTip
data.value: Write a server stats line (FPS, players, uptime, memory) to the log every 30s. Handy when running from a terminal; adds noise to the log otherwise.

data.name: Main_ZombieCheckTip
data.value: When the REST API stays unreachable or the world stays frozen for this many checks in a row, restart the server. Turn off if your REST API is flaky and you'd rather it never auto-restart.

data.name: Main_ZombieThresholdTip
data.value: How many failed health checks in a row before the server is treated as wedged and force-restarted.

data.name: Main_RestCheckEveryTip
data.value: How often (seconds) the launcher polls the REST API for status/health. 5-10 is recommended. Going below 5 just hammers the API for no real gain (allowed, but not advised); it won't go below 1. Applies on next start.

data.name: Main_ServerCommandsTip
data.value: Send live commands to the running server: announce, kick / ban / unban, save, shutdown. Available while the server is up.

data.name: Main_MultiServerMessage
data.value: Detected {0} Palworld servers running from this folder. They share one save and set of ports, so they'll conflict, the launcher manages a single server. Shut them all down, or exit.

data.name: Main_ServerRunningMessage
data.value:
A Palworld server started from this folder is already running, almost certainly yours from a previous session, since the launcher only tracks servers it started here.
Reconnect to keep managing it over the REST API (live stats, scheduled restarts, backups; the Server Log tab stays empty for a server the launcher didn't start itself). Otherwise shut it down, or exit.

data.name: Main_InstallMessage
data.value:
This downloads and installs the Palworld dedicated server with SteamCMD, about 4 GB, into this launcher's folder. A SteamCMD window opens to show progress.
Download and install it now?

data.name: Main_ShutdownNowMessage
data.value: Skip the countdown and shut the server down now?

data.name: Main_ShutdownForceMessage
data.value: REST is not enabled, so the server can't be shut down gracefully. It will be force-stopped, the process is killed directly and the last autosave limits any loss. Turn on REST for graceful shutdowns.

data.name: Main_ShutdownChoiceMessage
data.value: Shut down now, or on a timer that shows players an in-game countdown first?

data.name: Main_TimedShutdownMessage
data.value: Players get an in-game countdown for this many seconds before the server shuts down.

data.name: Main_ServerInstalledMessage
data.value:
Server successfully installed.
Enable the REST API? It's what lets the launcher control the server: apply game updates, gracefully save and shut down, schedule restarts and backups, and monitor server health.
If you enable it, the launcher generates the server config now and sets a secure, randomly generated admin password (view or change it later under Server Settings, in the Admin tab). You can also skip this and enable it yourself later.

data.name: Main_EnableRestMessage
data.value:
This launcher is built around Palworld's REST API and uses it for almost everything: graceful save-and-shutdown, fresh backups, live health / zombie monitoring, automatic restarts, and player join/leave logging.
The REST API isn't enabled on this server yet, so those features will be limited, shutdowns fall back to a hard kill, backups may be stale, and there are no automatic restarts.
Enable it now with a secure, randomly-generated admin password?

data.name: Main_RestEnabledMessage
data.value: Done, the REST API is on with a new random admin password (view or change it under Server Settings, in the Admin tab). It takes effect the next time you start the server.

data.name: Main_RestFailedMessage
data.value: Couldn't write the server config. Make sure the server is installed and stopped, then set it manually under Server Settings, in the Admin tab.

data.name: Main_ServerStillRunningMessage
data.value: The Palworld server is still running. What should happen to it?

data.name: Main_ForceShutdownConfirmMessage
data.value: This will shut the server down immediately without a save by killing the process entirely. It can cause save data loss or corruption. This is not recommended unless the server has completely stopped responding.

data.name: Main_PortCheckConsentMessage
data.value: Your public IP address will be sent to check-host.cc, a free external service, to check whether players can reach your server from outside your network. Nothing except your public IP and the ports you test is sent to their service.

data.name: Main_UpdateRunningMessage
data.value: A newer server build ({0}) is available. Update and restart the server now? Players get the usual restart warnings.

data.name: Main_UpdateStoppedMessage
data.value: A newer server build ({0}) is available. Download and apply it now?

data.name: Times_EveryTip
data.value: Fill the whole day at this interval, stepping from midnight. Pick a preset or type a decimal like 2.5. Replaces the times below.

data.name: Times_MinuteTip
data.value: Any minute 0-59, pick a preset or type it

data.name: Times_InvalidIntervalMessage
data.value: Enter an interval between 15 minutes and 24 hours, e.g. 30m, 2h, or 2.5 (hours).

data.name: Times_ReplaceMessage
data.value: This replaces the {0} time(s) below with a schedule every {1}. Continue?

data.name: App_ErrorFormat
PortCheck_Intro
data.value: Checks whether your ports can be reached from the internet, using check-host.cc. Run this with the server stopped so the launcher can bind and test the ports.

data.name: PortCheck_FirewallNote
data.value: This test binds a temporary listener on the launcher. If Windows Firewall prompts, allow it, otherwise ports can falsely show as unreachable. REST and RCON are admin ports: they should normally NOT be reachable from the internet, so a green result there is a warning, not a pass.

data.name: PortCheck_NoPublicIpMessage
data.value: Enter your public IP first (it's normally filled in automatically).

data.name: PortCheck_ServiceUnavailable
data.value: Unavailable, can't reach check-host.cc right now.

data.name: PortCheck_Failed
data.value: Port check failed, see the log.

data.name: ServerCmd_UnbanHint
data.value: Unban must use the platform user id (e.g. steam_0123...).

data.name: ServerCmd_RestOff
data.value: REST API is off or not responding.

data.name: ServerCmd_EnterUserId
data.value: Enter a user id to unban.

data.name: ServerCmd_NoUserIdTooltip
data.value: No user id reported for this player.

data.name: ServerCmd_BanConfirm
data.value: Ban {0} ({1})? They'll be kicked and blocked from rejoining.

data.name: Discord_BlurbPrefix
data.value: Control the server from Discord with slash commands, and/or post lifecycle notifications to a channel. Create a bot and copy its token from the

data.name: Discord_BlurbSuffix
data.value: (add a Bot, then invite it with the bot + applications.commands scopes).

data.name: Discord_SecurityNote
data.value: Security: set a command channel and/or a required role, the bot only acts when every gate you set passes (set at least one). Anyone who can post in the command channel, or has the role, can control the server, so lock it down (a private, admin-only channel and/or an admin role). Enable Discord Developer Mode, then right-click a channel or role -> Copy ID. The token is stored locally in launcher.json and is never logged.

data.name: Discord_CommandsExposedHint
data.value: Tick the slash commands the bot should accept. Destructive ones (restart, stop, kick, ban) are off by default. Changes take effect on Save (the bot reconnects and re-registers its commands).

data.name: Discord_NotifyLifecycle
data.value: Notify on up / down / update / crash

data.name: Discord_NotifyPlayers
data.value: Notify on player join / leave (needs REST)

data.name: Discord_InvalidChannelIdMessage
data.value: The command channel ID must be a number (Discord Developer Mode -> right-click the channel -> Copy Channel ID).

data.name: Discord_InvalidRoleIdMessage
data.value: The required role ID must be a number (Discord Developer Mode -> right-click the role -> Copy Role ID).

data.name: Discord_BotNotReadyMessage
data.value: To enable the control bot, set a bot token and at least one gate: a command channel ID and/or a required role ID.

data.name: Mods_Disclaimer
data.value: Only mods that are built to run on servers will work here. Many mods are client-side only and can't or shouldn't be installed on the server, so check a mod's description before loading it. Use mods at your own risk, they may cause save-data corruption or crashes.

data.name: Mods_SteamAccountNote
data.value: Only needed to download Workshop mods, dropped-in mods need no account. Steam's own SteamCMD handles the sign-in in its own window, so the launcher never sees or stores your password, it only remembers your username.

data.name: Mods_AddByIdLabel
data.value: Add by Steam Workshop id or URL:

data.name: Mods_NoAccountWarning
data.value: You have Workshop mods enabled but no Steam account connected. Connect one above to download them, or they'll be skipped on start (dropped-in mods still load).

data.name: Mods_LoosePakDescription
data.value: You don't need this if you're using Steam Workshop above. For raw .pak mods with no Info.json (many NexusMods downloads): drop them into the loose-paks folder and toggle them here. They're enabled/disabled by renaming (never deleted) and load on the next server start.

data.name: Mods_EnterUsernameBody
data.value: Enter your Steam account name first, then connect.

data.name: Mods_SteamCmdWindowOpened
data.value: A SteamCMD window opened. Enter your password and Steam Guard code there, it stays open so you can read the result, then close it.

data.name: Mods_ConfirmSignInFailed
data.value: Couldn't confirm the sign-in. Connect again and watch the SteamCMD window for the error (wrong password or Steam Guard code).

data.name: Mods_SavedSignInInvalid
data.value: Your saved sign-in isn't valid anymore, connect again.

data.name: Mods_InvalidIdBody
data.value: Enter a numeric Steam Workshop id, or a workshop URL like https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=123456.

data.name: Mods_AlreadyAddedBody
data.value: That Workshop id is already in the list.

data.name: Mods_NotPalworldBody
data.value: This item's Steam app id is {0}, not Palworld's ({1}), so it may not work on your server. Add it anyway?

data.name: Mods_ScanNoneFound
data.value: No new mods found in the mods folder.

data.name: Mods_ScanAdded
data.value: Added {0} mod(s) from the mods folder.

data.name: Mods_ScanSkipped
data.value: Skipped {0} folder(s) with no readable Info.json.

data.name: Mods_NoAccountSaveBody
data.value: You have Workshop mods enabled but no Steam account connected, so they can't be downloaded and will be skipped when the server starts (dropped-in mods still load). Save anyway?

data.name: Mods_PackageUnresolvedTip
data.value: Package name not resolved yet (download or scan the mod).

data.name: Mods_OpenWorkshopPageTip
data.value: Open this mod's Steam Workshop page.

data.name: Mods_DeleteModTip
data.value: Delete this mod (removes it from the list and deletes its downloaded files).

data.name: Mods_EmptyList
data.value: No mods yet. Add a Workshop id above, or drop mod folders into the mods folder and Scan.

data.name: Mods_DeleteBody
data.value:
This will delete the mod files and remove it from your mod list.
{0}

data.name: Mods_DeleteFailedBody
data.value: Removed it from the list, but couldn't delete the files: {0}

data.name: Mods_LoosePakEmpty
data.value: No loose .pak mods found. Drop .pak files (with any .utoc / .ucas) into the loose-paks folder, then Rescan.

data.name: Mods_Ue4ssNotInstalledBody
data.value: UE4SS isn't installed yet. Add a UE4SS mod from the Workshop and start the server once, then its mods folder will appear here.

data.name: Settings_WorldBlurb
data.value: Gameplay and balance, difficulty, EXP / capture / drop rates, damage multipliers, and world features. Full reference:

data.name: Settings_AdminBlurb
data.value: Server management, server name, passwords, player limit, and the REST / RCON APIs. Full reference:

data.name: Settings_BannerServerRunning
data.value: The server is running, so the game-settings tabs are read-only (a running server overwrites its own config). Launch Arguments can still be edited, and apply on the next start just like the game settings.

data.name: Settings_BannerGameUnavailable
data.value: Game settings unavailable, install the server first (no DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini found).

data.name: Settings_UndocumentedBlurb
data.value: Settings the official docs don't cover. Each tooltip has our best guess, but we can't vouch for these, some may be internal or unused. Look them up in the

data.name: Settings_UndocNoneRecognized
data.value: None, every key in your config is recognized.

data.name: Settings_NoEffectTitle
data.value: This setting does nothing on a server

data.name: Settings_NoEffectMessage
data.value: Heads up: '{0}' is a client / single-player setting and has no effect on a dedicated server. Changing it here won't change how the server plays. Use the individual settings (or the Difficulty preset buttons at the top of the Game Settings tab) to control server difficulty.

data.name: Settings_PresetNoChanges
data.value: Your settings already match the {0} preset, nothing to change.

data.name: Settings_PresetConfirmMessage
data.value:
These are the values as of Palworld 1.0. Some settings may have been added or changed since.
Here are the values that will change for the {0} preset:
{1}
Any values not listed above will remain the same.
Apply and save these changes?

data.name: Settings_PresetAppliedMessage
data.value: Applied the {0} preset. {1} setting(s) changed and saved.

data.name: Settings_LaunchArgsIntro
data.value: The exact command line used to launch the dedicated server (rebuilt live below). Where an argument here and a PalWorldSettings.ini setting control the same thing, the argument wins, a field left blank, 0, or at its default is left off, so the ini value is used instead. Most game settings have no argument and live only in the ini (edit those under Server Settings). Changes take effect on the next start.

data.name: Settings_ListenPortNote
data.value: is the port the server binds to and players connect to.

data.name: Settings_PublicPortNote
data.value: (community servers only) sets what the server advertises to the public list, it does NOT change the listen port.

data.name: Settings_TipQueryPort
data.value: The Steam query port. Blank or 0 auto-picks the first free port from 27015 (so several servers on one PC don't collide). Set a fixed one if you forward it.

data.name: Settings_TipListenPort
data.value: The UDP port the server binds to, what players connect to (Palworld default 8211). Always applied. This is not the Public port below.

data.name: Settings_TipMaxPlayers
data.value: Overrides ServerPlayerMaxNum in the ini when set. 0 = leave the argument off and use the game setting.

data.name: Settings_TipPerfThreads
data.value: Adds -useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS. Recommended on for a dedicated server.

data.name: Settings_TipWorkerThreads
data.value: -NumberOfWorkerThreadsServer. 0 = auto (left off). Only used when Performance threads is on.

data.name: Settings_TipCommunity
data.value: -publiclobby: list the server on the public/community browser. Off = private (players join by IP).

data.name: Settings_TipPublicIp
data.value: -publicip for community servers (blank = auto-detect). Advertising only; does not change what the server binds to.

data.name: Settings_TipPublicPort
data.value: -publicport for community servers: the external port advertised to the public list. Does NOT change the listen port. 0 = left off (use the ini value).

data.name: Settings_TipLogFormat
data.value: -logformat (Text or Json). Blank = left off; the ini/default applies.

data.name: Settings_ExtraArgsNote
data.value: Raw arguments appended to the command line verbatim (space- or line-separated). Some Unreal servers accept map / game-class overrides here. Only change this if you know what you're doing.

data.name: Settings_AdvancedIntro
data.value: Low-level settings applied to the server process after it launches. The wrong values here can hurt performance, change only if you know what you're doing. Takes effect on the next start.

data.name: Settings_TipPriority
data.value: Windows priority for the server process. Above normal / High can help under load but can starve other apps and hurt performance; RealTime isn't offered (unsafe).

data.name: Settings_CpuAffinityNote
data.value: CPU affinity, pin the server to specific cores (all checked = no restriction / use every core):

data.name: Settings_AdvancedArgsMessage
data.value: You've put custom arguments in the Advanced box. They're passed to the server exactly as typed, only keep them if you know what you're doing.

data.name: Settings_ConfirmSaveMessage
Settings_NotSavedMessage
data.value: '{0}' has a value that would break the config format (a stray comma, quote, or unbalanced parenthesis). Nothing was saved, fix it and try again.

data.name: Settings_ResetFieldTooltip
data.value: Reset this field to its default

data.name: Settings_ResetAllMessage
data.value: Reset every field in this panel to its default value? You'll still need to Save to apply.

data.name: Cat_ServerPassword_Desc
data.value: 서버에 접속할 때 필요한 비밀번호입니다. 비워두면 비밀번호가 없습니다.

data.name: Cat_AdminPassword_Desc
data.value: 서버 관리자 권한을 얻는 데 사용되는 비밀번호입니다. REST API와 RCON을 사용하려면 필요합니다.

data.name: Cat_ServerPlayerMaxNum_Desc
data.value: 서버에 접속할 수 있는 최대 플레이어 수입니다. -players 실행 인자가 설정되어 있으면 이 값을 덮어씁니다.

data.name: Cat_CoopPlayerMaxNum_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 커뮤니티에서는 협동 플레이 파티 인원수로 추측하지만, 데디케이트 서버에는 영향을 주지 않는 내부 값일 수 있습니다.

data.name: Cat_RESTAPIEnabled_Desc
data.value: REST API를 활성화합니다. 런처가 통계 조회, 정상 종료, 플레이어 로그 기록을 하려면 이 기능이 필요합니다.

data.name: Cat_BanListURL_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 서버가 대조하는 밴 목록으로 추정되지만, 정확한 형식과 동작 방식은 확인되지 않았습니다.

data.name: Cat_bIsUseBackupSaveData_Desc
data.value: Palworld 자체의 월드 백업 기능을 활성화합니다(런처의 백업 기능과는 별개입니다). 활성화하면 디스크 부하가 늘어납니다.

data.name: Cat_bIsShowJoinLeftMessage_Desc
data.value: 데디케이트 서버에서 플레이어가 접속하거나 퇴장할 때 게임 내 메시지를 표시합니다.

data.name: Cat_bAllowClientMod_Desc
data.value: 모드를 활성화한 플레이어가 서버에 접속할 수 있도록 허용합니다.

data.name: Cat_bIsMultiplay_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 월드 생성 시 게임 클라이언트에 표시되는 '멀티플레이: 예/아니오' 옵션으로 추정되며, 데디케이트 서버에는 영향을 주지 않습니다. 기본값은 False입니다.

data.name: Cat_Region_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 일종의 지역 코드로 보이지만, 예상 형식(NA, US, 숫자 등)과 용도(매치메이킹, 서버 브라우저, 혹은 둘 다 아닌지)가 모두 알려져 있지 않습니다. 기본값은 비어 있습니다.

data.name: Cat_CrossplayPlatforms_Desc
data.value: 서버에 접속할 수 있도록 허용된 플랫폼을 튜플 형태로 지정합니다. 기본값: (Steam,Xbox,PS5,Mac).

data.name: Cat_PublicIP_Desc
data.value: 커뮤니티 서버: 외부에 공개되는 공인 IP를 직접 지정합니다. 비워두면 자동으로 감지합니다.

data.name: Cat_PublicPort_Desc
data.value: 커뮤니티 서버: 외부에 공개되는 공인 포트를 직접 지정합니다. 서버의 수신 포트 자체는 변경되지 않습니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnableBuildingPlayerUIdDisplay_Desc
data.value: 건축물에 제작자의 플레이어 ID를 표시할지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_BuildingNameDisplayCacheTTLSeconds_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. '건축물에 제작자 ID 표시' 기능에서 캐시된 건축물 표시 이름이 갱신되기 전까지 유지되는 시간으로 추정됩니다(초).

data.name: Cat_BaseCampMaxNum_Desc
data.value: 맵에 존재할 수 있는 거점의 총 최대 개수입니다(모든 길드의 거점을 합산).

data.name: Cat_BaseCampMaxNumInGuild_Desc
data.value: 길드당 보유할 수 있는 최대 거점 수입니다. 기본값은 4이며(최대 10), 값을 늘리면 처리 부하가 증가합니다.

data.name: Cat_BaseCampWorkerMaxNum_Desc
data.value: 거점당 배치할 수 있는 최대 팰 수입니다(최대 50). 값을 늘리면 처리 부하가 증가합니다.

data.name: Cat_MaxBuildingLimitNum_Desc
data.value: 플레이어 1인당 건축물 개수 제한입니다(0 = 제한 없음).

data.name: Cat_DropItemMaxNum_Label
data.value: 월드 내의 드롭 아이템 최대 수

data.name: Cat_DropItemMaxNum_Desc
data.value: 월드에 떨어진 아이템이 소멸되기 시작하는 기준이 되는 최대 개수입니다.

data.name: Cat_PhysicsActiveDropItemMaxNum_Label
data.value: 월드 내의 물리 거동 드롭 아이템 최대 수

data.name: Cat_PhysicsActiveDropItemMaxNum_Desc
data.value: 물리 효과가 적용될 수 있는 드롭 아이템의 최대 개수입니다. -1 = 제한 없음. 값을 늘리면 처리 부하에 영향을 줄 수 있습니다.

data.name: Cat_DropItemMaxNum_UNKO_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 사용되지 않는 것으로 보이며, 남아 있는 불필요한 값이거나 아직 구현되지 않은 기능으로 추정됩니다.

data.name: Cat_ServerReplicatePawnCullDistance_Desc
data.value: 플레이어를 기준으로 팰을 동기화하는 거리입니다(cm). 최소 5000, 최대 15000.

data.name: Cat_ItemContainerForceMarkDirtyInterval_Desc
data.value: 컨테이너 UI가 열려 있는 동안 강제로 재동기화하는 주기입니다(초).

data.name: Cat_MaxGuildsPerFrame_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 서버가 한 틱(프레임)당 처리하는 길드 수를 제한하는 값으로 추정되며, 성능 조절용 설정으로 보입니다.

data.name: Cat_PlayerDataPalStorageUpdateCheckTickInterval_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 서버가 플레이어의 팰박스/보관함 데이터 변경 여부를 확인하여 동기화하는 주기로 추정됩니다(초).

data.name: Cat_Difficulty_Desc
data.value: 클라이언트/싱글 플레이용 난이도 프리셋입니다. 데디케이트 서버에는 영향을 주지 않으므로 None으로 두고, 아래의 개별 설정 항목(또는 상단의 난이도 프리셋 버튼)을 사용해 서버 난이도를 설정하십시오.

data.name: Cat_bHardcore_Label
data.value: 하드코어 모드: 플레이어 사망 시 영구적 손실

data.name: Cat_bHardcore_Desc
data.value: 하드코어 모드를 활성화합니다. 사망 시 리스폰할 수 없습니다.

data.name: Cat_bCharacterRecreateInHardcore_Desc
data.value: 하드코어 모드에서 사망 시 캐릭터를 다시 생성할 수 있는지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_bIsPvP_Desc
data.value: PvP를 활성화합니다. '플레이어 간 피해'와 '다른 길드에 대한 거점 방어'도 함께 켜야 실제로 적용됩니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnablePlayerToPlayerDamage_Desc
data.value: 플레이어끼리 서로 피해를 입힐 수 있도록 합니다. PvP를 구성하는 세 가지 설정 중 하나입니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnableFriendlyFire_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 커뮤니티에서는 플레이어와 팰이 같은 길드원에게 피해를 줄 수 있게 하는 설정이라고 알려져 있습니다.

data.name: Cat_bCanPickupOtherGuildDeathPenaltyDrop_Desc
data.value: 다른 길드 플레이어가 사망 시 떨어뜨린 팰과 아이템을 주울 수 있도록 허용합니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnableDefenseOtherGuildPlayer_Desc
data.value: 거점의 팰이 거점에 침입한 적대적인 플레이어를 공격합니다. PvP를 구성하는 세 가지 설정 중 하나입니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnableFastTravelOnlyBaseCamp_Desc
data.value: 빠른 이동을 거점 간 이동으로만 제한합니다.

data.name: Cat_EnablePredatorBossPal_Desc
data.value: 월드에 포식자 팰이 출현할 수 있도록 허용합니다.

data.name: Cat_bBuildAreaLimit_Desc
data.value: 빠른 이동 지점 등 특정 구조물 근처에서는 건축을 제한합니다.

data.name: Cat_bExistPlayerAfterLogout_Desc
data.value: 로그아웃 시 플레이어가 현재 위치에서 수면 상태로 남아 있을지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnableNonLoginPenalty_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 로그인하지 않은 동안 팰에게 디버프가 적용되는지와 관련된 설정으로 추정되나, 테스트되지 않았습니다.

data.name: Cat_bIsStartLocationSelectByMap_Desc
data.value: 플레이어가 시작 위치를 직접 선택할 수 있도록 허용할지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_bActiveUNKO_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 사용되지 않는 것으로 보이며, 남아 있는 불필요한 값이거나 아직 구현되지 않은 기능으로 추정됩니다.

data.name: Cat_bEnableAimAssistPad_Desc
data.value: 컨트롤러용 조준 보정 기능입니다. 비활성화하려면 False로 설정하십시오.

data.name: Cat_bEnableAimAssistKeyboard_Desc
data.value: 마우스 및 키보드용 조준 보정 기능을 활성화합니다.

data.name: Cat_bAllowEnhanceStat_Weight_Desc
data.value: 소지 중량 스탯 포인트 분배를 허용합니다.

data.name: Cat_bAllowEnhanceStat_WorkSpeed_Desc
data.value: 작업 속도 스탯 포인트 분배를 허용합니다.

data.name: Cat_bAllowGlobalPalboxExport_Label
data.value: Allow Pal Genetic Data to be Saved in the Global Palbox

data.name: Cat_bAllowGlobalPalboxImport_Label
data.value: Allow Pals to be Reconstructed from the Global Palbox

data.name: Cat_bAutoResetGuildNoOnlinePlayers_Label
data.value: 일정 시간 내 로그인 기록이 없는 길드의 시설을 자동 삭제

data.name: Cat_bAutoResetGuildNoOnlinePlayers_Desc
data.value: 길드원이 아무도 로그인하지 않으면 건축물과 거점의 팰을 자동으로 삭제합니다.

data.name: Cat_AutoResetGuildTimeNoOnlinePlayers_Label
data.value: 로그인 기록이 없는 길드 시설의 자동 삭제까지 남은 시간(h)

data.name: Cat_AutoResetGuildTimeNoOnlinePlayers_Desc
data.value: 비활성 길드가 자동으로 초기화되기까지 걸리는 오프라인 시간입니다(시간). '비활성 길드 자동 초기화'가 켜져 있지 않으면 무시됩니다.

data.name: Cat_bDisplayPvPItemNumOnWorldMap_BaseCamp_Label
data.value: Show base PvP items on map

data.name: Cat_bDisplayPvPItemNumOnWorldMap_BaseCamp_Desc
data.value: 각 거점에 있는 PvP 전용 아이템 수를 맵에 표시할지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_bDisplayPvPItemNumOnWorldMap_Player_Label
data.value: Show player PvP items on map

data.name: Cat_bDisplayPvPItemNumOnWorldMap_Player_Desc
data.value: 플레이어 위치와 PvP 전용 아이템 수를 맵에 표시할지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_bIsRandomizerPalLevelRandom_Label
data.value: 무작위 모드에서 야생 팰의 레벨을 완전히 무작위화한다

data.name: Cat_bIsRandomizerPalLevelRandom_Desc
data.value: 켜면 야생 팰의 레벨이 완전히 무작위로 결정됩니다. 끄면 각 지역에 지정된 범위 내에서 레벨이 무작위로 정해집니다.

data.name: Cat_RandomizerType_Desc
data.value: 팰 출현 랜덤화 방식입니다. None = 랜덤화 없음, Region = 지역별로 랜덤화, All = 완전 무작위.

data.name: Cat_RandomizerSeed_Desc
data.value: 팰 출현 랜덤화가 활성화되었을 때 사용되는 시드 값입니다.

data.name: Cat_VoiceChatMaxVolumeDistance_Desc
data.value: 음성 채팅 음량이 감쇠하지 않는 거리입니다.

data.name: Cat_PalSpawnNumRate_Label
data.value: 팰 출현 배율 ※게임 성능에 영향을 미칩니다

data.name: Cat_PalSpawnNumRate_Desc
data.value: 팰 출현 배율입니다. 성능에 영향을 줍니다.

data.name: Cat_PalEggDefaultHatchingTime_Label
data.value: 거대알 부화에 걸리는 시간(h) ※ 다른 알도 상응하여 부화 시간이 변경됩니다.

data.name: Cat_PalEggDefaultHatchingTime_Desc
data.value: 거대한 알이 부화하는 데 걸리는 시간입니다(시간). 다른 알들도 부화까지 시간이 필요합니다.

data.name: Cat_WorkSpeedRate_Desc
data.value: 플레이어와 팰이 작업을 완료하는 속도입니다. 배율 값으로, 2.0이면 작업 속도가 두 배가 됩니다.

data.name: Cat_MonsterFarmActionSpeedRate_Desc
data.value: 방목을 통한 아이템 생산 속도 배율입니다.

data.name: Cat_PlayerAutoHpRegeneRateInSleep_Label
data.value: 플레이어 수면 시 HP 회복 배율

data.name: Cat_PlayerAutoHpRegeneRateInSleep_Desc
data.value: 플레이어 수면 중 체력 회복 배율입니다.

data.name: Cat_PalAutoHpRegeneRateInSleep_Label
data.value: 팰 수면 시 HP 회복 배율(팰 상자 내 HP 회복 배율)

data.name: Cat_PalAutoHpRegeneRateInSleep_Desc
data.value: 팰박스에서 수면 중인 팰의 체력 회복 배율입니다.

data.name: Cat_DropItemAliveMaxHours_Desc
data.value: 드롭된 아이템이 소멸되기까지 월드에 남아 있는 시간입니다(시간). 값을 낮추면 서버 정리 및 성능에 도움이 됩니다.

data.name: Cat_BuildObjectHpRate_Desc
data.value: 건축물 체력 배율입니다. 2.0이면 건축물 체력이 두 배가 됩니다.

data.name: Cat_DeathPenalty_Desc
data.value: 사망 시 드롭되는 항목입니다. None = 아무것도 드롭하지 않음, Item = 장비를 제외한 모든 아이템, ItemAndEquipment = 모든 아이템, All = 모든 아이템과 파티의 모든 팰.

data.name: Cat_bPalLost_Label
data.value: 하드코어 모드: 팰 빈사 시 영구적 손실

data.name: Cat_BlockRespawnTime_Desc
data.value: 사망 후 리스폰할 수 있을 때까지의 대기 시간입니다(초).

data.name: Cat_RespawnPenaltyDurationThreshold_Desc
data.value: 생존 시간 기준값입니다(초). 이 시간만큼 생존하지 못하고 다시 사망하면 아래의 리스폰 대기 시간 배율이 적용됩니다.

data.name: Cat_GuildRejoinCooldownMinutes_Desc
data.value: 길드에 재가입할 수 있을 때까지의 대기 시간입니다(분).

data.name: Cat_DenyTechnologyList_Desc
data.value: 특정 테크놀로지를 비활성화합니다. 테크놀로지 ID를 지정하십시오. 예: ("PALBOX", "RepairBench"). 비워두면 없음.

data.name: Cat_bAdditionalDropItemWhenPlayerKillingInPvPMode_Desc
data.value: PvP가 활성화된 상태에서 플레이어가 사망했을 때 특수 아이템을 드롭할지 여부입니다.

data.name: Cat_AdditionalDropItemWhenPlayerKillingInPvPMode_Desc
data.value: PvP 킬 시 드롭할 아이템의 ID입니다('PvP 킬: 아이템 드롭'이 켜져 있을 때 사용됩니다).

data.name: Cat_AdditionalDropItemNumWhenPlayerKillingInPvPMode_Desc
data.value: PvP 킬 시 드롭할 아이템의 수량입니다('PvP 킬: 아이템 드롭'이 켜져 있을 때 사용됩니다).

data.name: Cat_SupplyDropSpan_Label
data.value: 운석 및 보급 물자 투하 간격 (분)

data.name: Cat_AutoTransferMasterCheckIntervalSeconds_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 서버가 비활성 상태인 길드장/거점 소유자로부터 소유권을 자동으로 이전해야 하는지 확인하는 주기로 추정됩니다(초).

data.name: Cat_AutoTransferMasterThresholdDays_Desc
data.value: 문서화되지 않음. 길드장/거점 소유자가 비활성 상태로 있어야 소유권이 자동으로 이전되기까지의 일수로 추정됩니다.

data.name: LauncherSettings_RestartPrompt
data.value: The launcher needs to restart to apply the new language ({0}). Restart now?

data.name: LauncherSettings_RestartPromptRunning
data.value: The launcher needs to restart to apply the new language ({0}). Your Palworld server keeps running, and the launcher reconnects to it. Restart now?

data.name: Settings_GameplayBlurb
data.value: Difficulty, EXP / capture / drop rates, day-night length, and world feature toggles. Full reference:

data.name: Settings_GameBalanceBlurb
data.value: Damage, drop, and stat multipliers that tune combat and progression balance. Full reference:

data.name: Settings_PerformanceBlurb
data.value: Limits that affect server load: base camps, guild size, object caps, and spawn density. Full reference:

data.name: Main_PinVersionTip
data.value: Freeze the server on its current build. While pinned, automatic updates are turned off. Uncheck to resume updating.

data.name: Main_AutomaticUpdatesTip
data.value: Download updates automatically. When off, the launcher won't update on start or while running. You can still check for updates manually.

data.name: LauncherSettings_AutoReconnect
data.value: Reconnect to a running server on launcher startup without asking

data.name: LauncherSettings_AutoReconnectTip
data.value: When exactly one managed server is found running at startup, adopt it without asking. If several are found, you'll still be prompted.

data.name: Main_ImportServerTip
data.value: Copy an existing Palworld dedicated server you installed elsewhere into this launcher. The original is left untouched.

data.name: Main_ImportPickTitle
data.value: Select your Palworld dedicated server folder (usually named PalServer)

data.name: Main_ImportInvalidMessage
data.value: That folder doesn't look like a Palworld dedicated server install. It's typically installed in a folder named PalServer. If you're using Steam, it's under Steam\steamapps\common\PalServer.

data.name: Main_ImportConfirmMessage
data.value: Copy this server into the launcher from '{0}'? The original is left in place, so once you've confirmed the launcher-managed copy works you can delete the original yourself.

data.name: Main_ImportFailedMessage
data.value: The import didn't complete. Check the log for details.

data.name: LauncherSettings_LoginOpen
data.value: Open the launcher and start the server at Windows login

data.name: LauncherSettings_LoginOpenTip
data.value: At login, opens the launcher and starts the server, so scheduled restarts, updates, backups, and health recovery run. No admin needed. Unchecking removes the Startup shortcut.

data.name: LauncherSettings_LoginOpenFailedMessage
data.value: The Startup shortcut couldn't be created or removed. Check the log for details.
